researcher.name: John Edward Mack, M.D.
researcher.other_notable_work: Pulitzer Prize 1977 for 'A Prince of Our Disorder' (T.E. Lawrence biography)

selection_criteria: Illustrates one or more central aspects of the phenomenon
Person willing to have story told (with or without pseudonym)
research_scope.inclusion_criterion: Whether experience was felt to be real by experiencer and communicated sincerely and authentically

second_book.title: Passport to the Cosmos: Human Transformation and Alien Encounters
second_book.structure: Thematic chapters rather than individual case studies

harvard_investigation.nature: Committee to review Mack's clinical care
harvard_investigation.significance: First time in Harvard history a tenured professor subjected to such investigation
harvard_investigation.outcome: August 1995: Committee reaffirmed Dr. Mack's academic freedom
harvard_investigation.outcome_detail: Freedom to study what he wishes and state opinions without impediment

archive_location.institution: Rice University, Woodson Research Center, Fondren Library
archive_location.materials: Reports, correspondence, email, interview recordings, photographs, videorecordings
archive_location.access: Stored offsite at Library Service Center; 24-hour notice required
archive_location.digital_status: Digitization in progress led by Karin Austin (JEMI director)
archive_location.restrictions: Private data restricted until anonymization or 2074
archive_location.aoti_founder: Jeffrey J. Kripal (Professor of Religion, Rice University)

ariel_school.description: Children observed craft and beings communicating telepathically about environmental stewardship
ariel_school.significance: Cross-cultural validation; consistent with North American reports

mit_conference.significance: Major academic conference on abduction phenomena

common_patterns_identified: Episodes of paralysis followed by perceived transport
